title: Software Engineer III
description:
About Team:
   

  

 The Acquisition and Discovery Engineering team at Walmart provides a seamless and delightful shopping experience to millions of shoppers worldwide. As a software engineer in this team, you will have the opportunity to work on top of the funnel shopping experience – Homepage, Content Pages for SEO and SEM, Search \\& Browse experiences, Deals and Item pages across mobile and web platforms.
   

  

 This collaborative team made up of Engineering, UX, Product Management, Business and Analytics is rapidly iterating on new products and site features at scale. We deliver high performing solutions on our next generation front\\-end platform. Virtual Try\\-On, Gen AI integration, personalized complete\\-the\\-look, Video shopping, etc are just a few examples of the features developed by this team in collaboration with many other teams. Continuous experimentation, innovation and feedback by millions of customers makes this work even more exciting!
   

  

 What You Will Do:
   

  

 Develop and deliver feature sets involving responsive, high\\-performance web UIs integrated with RESTful services, ensuring a seamless user experience.
   

  

 Build scalable front\\-end applications using React, TypeScript, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS, following modern development practices.
   

  

 Collaborate with UX designers, product owners, and business stakeholders to analyze requirements and deliver sustainable, scalable technical solutions aligned with business goals.
   

  

 Contribute to software design and architecture by applying engineering and design patterns; work closely with peers, senior engineers, and architects.
   

  

 Drive the development and enhancement of product components, ensuring alignment with enterprise architecture, design, and development standards.
   

  

 Build reusable, modular React components using TypeScript and CSS modules.
   

  

 Write comprehensive unit and integration tests using tools like Jest to ensure code quality and reliability.
   

  

 Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks using tools such as Chrome DevTools, Lighthouse, and WebPageTest.
   

  

 Develop interactive, data\\-driven UI components, including graphs and charts, handling complex and large\\-scale data structures.
   

  

 Extend and improve open\\-source tools and frameworks where applicable.
   

  

 Follow industry best practices and adopt new tools, technologies, and methodologies to improve agility and development efficiency.
   

  

 Stay current with evolving web technologies, frameworks, and best practices, and share knowledge across teams.
   

  

 Drive operational excellence by ensuring high availability, performance, and reliability of production systems using AI\\-driven monitoring, alerting, and incident management.
   

  

 Champion engineering excellence through best practices such as code reviews, design reviews, and CI/CD, leveraging AI\\-assisted development tools to enhance productivity and code quality.
   

  

 Implement AI\\-powered solutions for proactive issue detection, automated root cause analysis, and self\\-healing systems to reduce downtime and improve resilience.
   

  

 Define and track engineering and operational metrics, using AI/analytics to enable continuous improvement and data\\-driven decisions.
   

  

 Foster a culture of innovation by adopting modern AI technologies, encouraging experimentation, and continuously improving development and operational workflows.
   

  

 What You Will Bring:
   

  

 Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field (or equivalent experience).
   

  

 2–6 years of experience in frontend/web development, preferably in consumer\\-facing or e\\-commerce platforms.
   

  

 Strong hands\\-on experience building scalable, high\\-performance, responsive web applications.
   

  

 Deep expertise in ReactJS, Redux, TypeScript, JavaScript (ES6\\+), HTML, CSS, and modern frontend development practices.
   

  

 Strong understanding of React fundamentals including Virtual DOM, component lifecycle, hooks, and state management.
   

  

 Experience building reusable, customizable, and dynamic UI components using modern frontend frameworks and patterns.
   

  

 Proficiency in implementing design patterns and best practices, including asynchronous programming (Promises, observables), event handling, and application flow control.
   

  

 Experience developing and consuming RESTful APIs and/or GraphQL services using Node.js, Rust, or Go is a plus.
   

  

 Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, automated testing frameworks, and frontend testing strategies.
   

  

 Strong understanding of browser internals, rendering, and performance optimization techniques.
   

  

 Experience with development tools such as Git, NPM, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenObserve or Splunk.
   

  

 Solid understanding of SDLC best practices, including code reviews, version control, testing, and deployment processes.
   

  

 Experience with containerization and orchestration tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) and microservices\\-based architectures.
   

  

 Exposure to cloud platforms and deploying web applications in cloud environments.
   

  

 Experience working with large\\-scale distributed systems and fully automated CI/CD environments is a plus.
   

  

 Strong analytical, problem\\-solving, and decision\\-making skills.
   

  

 Effective communication skills with the ability to articulate technical concepts and collaborate across teams.
   

  

 Proven troubleshooting skills, including root cause analysis and resolution of production issues, with a focus on long\\-term fixes and system stability.
   

  

**About Walmart Global Tech**
 Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people\\-led and tech\\-empowered.
   

  

 We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.
   

  

 Flexible, hybrid work
   

  

 Walmart’s culture sets us apart, and we know being together helps us innovate, learn and grow great careers. This role is based in our \\[Bangalore/Chennai] office for daily work, with the flexibility for associates to manage their personal lives.
   

  

**Benefits**
 Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best\\-in\\-class benefits maternity and parental leave, pto, health benefits, and much more.
   

  

 Belonging
   

  

 We aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.
   

  

 At Walmart, our vision is "everyone included." by fostering a workplace culture where everyone is—and feels—included, everyone wins. Our associates and customers reflect the makeup of all 19 countries where we operate. By making Walmart a welcoming place where all people feel like they belong, we’re able to engage associates, strengthen our business, improve our ability to serve customers, and support the communities where we operate.
   

  

 Equal opportunity employer
   

  

 Walmart, inc., is an equal opportunities employer – by choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions – while being inclusive of all people.

With a rich legacy spanning over fifty years, the company has established itself as a pioneer in delivering innovative software solutions that streamline the entire lifecycle of construction and infrastructure projects. Listed on the MDAX and TecDAX stock exchanges in Germany, Nemetschek Group operates through multiple brands, including Nemetschek India, and is committed to driving technological advancements that enable clients to plan, build, manage, and operate their projects more efficiently. The company's mission is to empower professionals with intelligent, user\\-centric tools that foster collaboration, efficiency, and sustainability in the built environment.
   

  

**About The Role**

In this role, you will be instrumental in designing, developing, and maintaining intuitive user interfaces for Allplan’s desktop applications, primarily utilizing C\\#/WPF. Your contributions will directly impact the user experience, ensuring that our solutions are both visually appealing and highly functional. This position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals eager to grow their skills in UI development, collaborate with cross\\-functional teams, and contribute to innovative projects that shape the future of digital construction solutions.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 The ideal candidate will possess some professional or internship experience in UI or software development, preferably with a focus on C\\#/WPF. A foundational understanding of UI design principles and modern application development best practices is essential. Candidates should demonstrate a strong eagerness to learn, ask questions, and work collaboratively within a team environment. Attention to detail, especially concerning usability and visual consistency, is crucial. Effective communication skills and problem\\-solving abilities are highly valued, along with a desire for continuous growth in UI development. Proficiency in English is required; knowledge of additional languages will be considered an advantage.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 As a Junior Software Developer \\- UI, your key responsibilities will include:
   

  

* Implementing and maintaining UI components in C\\#/WPF, adhering to design and UX guidelines to ensure consistency and quality.

 Hands\\-on experience in building web based J2EE services/applications and Kafka, Apache Camel, RESTful Web\\-Services, Spring, Hibernate, Splunk, Caching.

 We use a hybrid way of working with primary in office presence coupled with an optimal mix of virtual presence. We use our campuses to collaborate and be together in person, as business needs require and for development and networking opportunities. This approach helps us make quicker decisions, remove location barriers across our global team, be more flexible in our personal lives.
   

  

**Benefits**
 Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best\\-in\\-class benefits maternity and parental leave, pto, health benefits, and much more.
   

  

 Belonging
   

  

 We aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.

 Equal opportunity employer

title: Performance Test Lead
description:
**About The Role**
 CargoFL is building the next generation of logistics technology, powering freight operations for a growing
   

  

 roster of enterprise clients. As our first Performance Test Lead, you will own and shape the entire
   

  

 performance engineering discipline — from strategy and tooling to execution and culture.
   

  

 This is a high\\-impact founding role. You will not be joining an existing team; you will be building one. Your
   

  

 work directly determines whether our TMS platform scales reliably under real\\-world production loads, and
   

  

 whether every release we ship is truly production\\-ready.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
 Performance Strategy \\& Leadership
   

  

* Own the end\\-to\\-end performance testing strategy across all CargoFL products and environments.
* Define KPIs, SLAs, and benchmarks for production readiness, load thresholds, and system stability.
* Build and scale the performance testing practice from the ground up, including processes, tooling, and


 — over time — headcount.
   

  

* Act as the internal authority on non\\-functional requirements (NFRs) and champion them throughout the


 SDLC.
   

  

 Test Design \\& Execution
   

  

* Design and execute comprehensive performance test suites: load, stress, soak, spike, volume, and


 concurrency tests.
   

  

* Build realistic, data\\-driven test scenarios modelling actual customer usage patterns across our 20\\+


 client tenants.
   

  

* Integrate performance tests into the CI/CD pipeline so every build is validated before it reaches staging


 or production.
   

  

* Validate API performance, database query efficiency, background job throughput, and end\\-to\\-end


 transaction latency.
   

  

 Production Readiness
   

  

* Own and maintain a production readiness checklist for all major releases.


 CargoFL Technologies \\| Confidential \\| Performance Test Lead — Job Description
   

  

* Define go/no\\-go criteria for deployments based on objective performance data.
* Collaborate with DevOps to set up and maintain realistic pre\\-prod environments capable of


 representative load simulation.
   

  

* Identify and triage performance regressions before they reach production; work with developers to


 resolve bottlenecks.
   

  

 Analysis \\& Reporting
   

  

* Analyse test results and translate findings into clear, actionable reports for both technical and non\\-


 technical stakeholders.
   

  

* Track performance trends over time; maintain a historical baseline and surface regressions early.
* Present load readiness status in sprint reviews and pre\\-release gates.


 Infrastructure \\& Tooling
   

  

* Evaluate, select, and maintain the performance testing toolchain (e.g., k6, JMeter, Gatling, Locust).
* Set up monitoring and observability dashboards to correlate test runs with system behaviour (CPU,


 memory, DB connections, GC).
   

  

* Work alongside DevOps/SRE on capacity planning and autoscaling validation.


**Required Qualifications \\& Skills**
**Must Have**
* 4\\+ years of hands\\-on performance / load testing experience in production\\-grade software systems.
* Strong proficiency with at least one major performance testing tool — k6, JMeter, Gatling, or Locust.
* Solid understanding of HTTP/REST APIs, databases (SQL), caching layers, and distributed systems.
* Experience integrating performance tests into CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab CI, or


 similar).
   

  

* Ability to read and profile code — you do not need to be a developer, but you need to understand where


 time is being spent.
   

  

* Strong analytical skills: profiling flame graphs, APM traces, slow query logs, and GC metrics.
* Clear, structured written and verbal communication — you will be writing reports for engineers and


 product leaders alike.
   

  

**Nice to Have**
* Experience in a logistics, supply chain, or enterprise SaaS environment.
* Familiarity with multi\\-tenant architecture and tenant\\-level performance isolation.
* Knowledge of observability tooling: Grafana, Prometheus, Datadog, New Relic, or equivalent.
* Exposure to chaos engineering / fault injection practices.
* Basic scripting ability in Python or JavaScript for test data generation and automation.
* Experience in hiring, mentoring, or leading a small QA or performance team.
